stdout:
[36m[Codex][0m Hold for Codex...


[36m[Codex][0m Hi, I’m Codex. I can pull context from the meeting and workspace, and I can turn decisions into concrete code changes or concise summaries.


[36m[Codex][0m Hi Alice, the last person name I see mentioned aloud was Priya. The line was: “Priya raised a concern about the approval policy.”


[36m[Codex][0m Main thread: launch is still blocked on Codex parity, with repeated concerns about the byte ceiling causing retry loops, caption reliability, and startup ordering.


[36m[Codex][0m Decisions or likely actions: keep rollout to 10% first, verify the marker-file crash path, write a fixture for the edge case, revoke the leaked token, check Sentry auth alerts, and coordinate with the mobile release branch. Testing gap noted: skipping live Meet leaves caption observer, meet.new resolution, and auto-leave paths untested, but the team considered that risk bounded.


[36m[Codex][0m Several people repeatedly said “Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path.” Carol also said “the Sentry alerts are firing again,” and Eve said “let me check Sentry one more time.”


[36m[Codex][0m Alice, Bob, Carol, Dan, and Eve have spoken so far.


[36m[Codex][0m 8–10 minutes ago, the main topic was launch risk around Codex parity: byte-ceiling retry behavior, testing gaps from skipping live Meet, and whether to narrow rollout to 10%.


[36m[Codex][0m Other repeated items were Sentry/auth alerts, revoking a leaked token, writing a fixture for an edge case, checking the marker-file crash path, and pulling up the GitHub issue/dashboard.


[36m[Codex][0m Yes. Alice said “I want to bring up the launch timeline,” Dan said “the launch is the priority this week,” and many people repeated that “the launch is blocked on the codex parity work.”


[36m[Codex][0m The meeting centered on launch readiness. The recurring conclusion was that launch is blocked on Codex parity work, with the team leaning toward a 10% rollout first.


[36m[Codex][0m Main risks discussed: Sentry alerts on the auth path, a leaked token that still needs revocation, flaky chat-hardening tests, caption reliability gaps, and a byte-ceiling issue that could cause repeated retries instead of narrowing results.


[36m[Codex][0m Technical direction: keep Postgres for production and SQLite for tests, use the marker-file approach because startup ordering makes env vars awkward, and confirm that path survives a crash. Action items mentioned: pull up the GitHub issue/dashboard, write a fixture for the edge case, coordinate with the mobile release branch, check Sentry, and verify the marker-file crash behavior.


[36m[Codex][0m Carol: “Postgres for prod SQLite for tests is the standard split.”


[36m[Codex][0m Alice also said: “Mohammed pinged me about the database pick.”
